pdn.desc: Site Plan Approval for two (2) residential towers above a 6-storey podium. Tower D is proposed at 45-storeys and features approximately 418 residential units, and Tower E is proposed at 49-storeys and features approximately 552 residential units. There will be approximately 75,270.28 square metres for an FSI of 10.17.

Proposal for a development of five new residential and mixed-use buildings with heights ranging from 13- to 49-storeys containing a total of 1,846 dwelling units and a new 2,734 square metre (0.27 hectare) public park. The proposed development contains a total gross floor area of 133,895 square metres, comprised of 133,330 square metres of residential gross floor area and 565 square metres of non-residential gross floor area resulting in a density of 4.99 times the area of the lot.

The application proposes to increase the permitted height and density from the approved heights of 35 and 37-storeys as per By-law 1263-2022 (OLT), to 46 and 58-storeys This height increase would result in a total gross floor area of 73,305 m2, comprised of 71,992 m2 of residential GFA and 1,313 m2 of non-residential GFA at-grade. A total of 1,206 residential units are proposed, including 553 units in the west tower and 653 in the east tower. Vehicular and loading access to the development continues to be proposed from the future east-west private laneway on-site, connecting Henning Avenue and Duplex Avenue. Three (3) levels of underground parking are proposed, containing a total of 216 vehicular parking spaces. The revised buildings maintain the setbacks, stepbacks, commercial uses, and heritage retention of the previous approval.

Official Plan Amendment & Rezoning for a mixed use development with 5 residential towers. A total of approximately 169,273.88 square metres of gross floor area is proposed, with density of approximately 8.98 FSI. A total of 2,328 residential units are proposed, of which 281 units to be rental units in Block 3, of which 30% will be available as affordable units. New public road and public park also proposed. See related applications: Subdivision File #: 23 165547 ESC 24 SB, Site Plans File #'s: 23 165545 ESC 24 SA; 23 165549 24 SA; and 23 165553 ESC 24 SA
Proposal for a 19-storey mixed use building and 7 storey podium with 185 units. Proposed residentail gross floor area is 14,642.9 m2, and retail gross floor area is 419.9 m2.

Draft Plan of Subdivision application to create 6 blocks to permit a 5 tower mixed-use development on the lands at 1710 Ellesmere Road, including two (2) blocks for public parkland and a new public road block. See Official Plan Amendment and Zoning By-law Amendment Application No. 23 165474 ESC 24 OZ.

To amend the Zoning By-law to lift the Holding provision (H) at 1-70 Eglinton Square, 1431and 1437 Victoria Park Avenue. This application is related to Zoning By-law Amendment Application No. 17 242390 ESC 35 OZ.

Proposal for a development includes three buildings: a 40-storey residential mixed-use building, a 21storey residential building, and a 6-storey office building, as well as a new private road and privately owned publiy accessible space (POPS). The Proposal will provide a total of 820 new residential units and 10,566 square metres of office gross floor area (GFA), 797 square metres of retail GFA, and 53,633 square metres of residential GFA, resulting in a net density of 5.3 times the area of the lot.

Official Plan Amendment application to add new policies for the subject site at 1960 Eglinton Ave E (Cosmetica lands).  The Site and Area Specific Policy (SASP) would permit a mixed-use development with a new street and parkland.  The development would consist of mixed-use buildings that range in height from 30 to 45 storeys and a six-storey non-residential building to replace and expand the gross floor area of the existing non-residential building (to be demolished).  The site is located within the Golden Mile Secondary Plan (GMSP) study area.

INACTIVE Site Plan applciation for a total of 40,886.39 square metres of gross floor area (GFA) over 50 storeys. A total of 461 residential units are proposed on Floors 9-50.

Site Plan approval application for a 40-storey tower with an 8-storey podium and three-levels of underground parking. The proposal incorporates residential, retail, and office uses, and will replace the existing commercial/retail spaces. The building includes 479 units, 164 vehicular parking spaces, and 504 bicycle parking spaces. The application is related to rezoning appeal, 22 176960 STE 12 OZ. The appeal has been settled and now includes a revised proposal for a 52-storey mixed use building.

Site Plan approval application for a 40-storey tower with an 8-storey podium and three-levels of underground parking. The proposal incorporates residential, retail, and office uses, and will replace the existing commercial/retail spaces. The building includes 479 units, 164 vehicular parking spaces, and 504 bicycle parking spaces. The application is related to rezoning appeal, 22 176960 STE 12 OZ. The appeal has been settled and now includes a revised proposal for a 52-storey mixed use building.

INACTIVE Proposed site plan approval to permit an existing 640 sq.ft. (59.5 sm), "Penguin Pick-Up (PPU)" building located on the retail/commercial lands at 1900 Eglinton Avenue East. The PPU is designed as a convenience pick-up facility for customers to retrieve on-line purchases. PPU staff retrieve the parcel from the PPU building and bring the item to customers who can park in the PPU designated parking area. The PPU is self-sufficient without the need to connect to utility services, such as stormwater or sewage, to operate. Hydro connection is provided via an existing light standard.

Zoning By-law Amendment application for the lands at 1-70 Eglinton Square, 1431 & 1437 Victoria Park Ave, 14, 18, 22 and 26 Engelhart Cres within the Golden Mile Secondary Plan Area to permit a mixed-use development. The application proposes six (6) tall buildings with building heights that range from 20 to 50 storeys and a six-storey mid-rise building. A total gross floor area of 318,654 sq. m (315,845 sq. m for residential uses and 2,809 sq. m for retail uses), 3,618 residential units and an FSI of 4.75 times the area of the lands are proposed. The existing three-storey residential buildings at 14, 18, 22 and 26 Engelhart Cres would be retained. The application is related to Official Plan Amendment application 16 230579 ESC 35 OZ (under appeal).

This is the first residential application (Block 12) for Site Plan Control related to the redevelopment of the former Celestica lands, now known as Crosstown.  This application proposes the construction of three towers of 32, 34 and 39 storeys atop a podium ranging from 2 to 5 storeys, a portion of which is the retained facade of the Parkin Building.  Also proposed on the site is an 8 storey mid-rise, and 18 three storey back-to-back townhouses. Eight if the 18 townhouses are proposed to be affordable ownership units.  A POPS connection is proposed through the site, connecting further public Street C with Block 14B, which will be open space. An underground garage will accommodate 957 vehicle parking spaces and 7 care-shares spaces.  1,260 bicycle parking spaces are also proposed, as well as one bike share station.

City Initiated Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment seeking to permit two buildings on site. The proposal is for a building fronting Queen Street West with 6 residential storeys and a partial 7th storey for enclosed mechanical penthouse and amenity space, and an 18-storey residential building (plus enclosed mechanical penthouse) fronting Eastern Avenue.  Taken together both buildings have a total residential gross floor area of 24,275 square metres, and a non-residential gross floor area of 1,112 square metres. A total of 328 residential rental dwelling units are proposed. The proposal includes a childcare space on the ground floor and one level of underground parking that includes 89 vehicular parking spaces and 331 bicycle parking spaces.

Applications for Zoning By-law Amendment and Site Plan Control have been revised and submitted. The initial proposal was for two towers measuring 24 storeys (90 Eglinton Ave W) and 32 storeys (50-60 Eglinton Ave W) in height. The proposal has now been amended to increase the height of the towers to 34 and 39 storeys in height atop a 3-storey mixed-use base building with 688 residential units. The development proposes a total gross floor area of 54,755 square metres, of which 2,898 square metres is proposed for retail uses and 4,048 square metres is proposed for office uses, resulting in an FSI of 13.73 times the lot area.

These spaces contribute to a total parking supply of 1,599 parking spaces on the site

Site Plan application for the approval of a 48-storey non-residential building (218.55 metres), inclusive of a 6-storey podium, having a gross floor area of 114,586 square metres. This application is related to the subdivision application 16 270078 STE 30 SB which is currently under review by staff.

Site Plan Control Application proposes the development of a 6-storey municipal shelter building containing a total of 36 sleeping rooms, comprising a mix of 1, 2, 3, and 4-bed rooms, for a total of 76 beds. A paved access for Wheel-Trans loading is proposed along Eglinton Avenue West, adjacent to the southern property line. In addition, the proposal includes 344.30 square metres of indoor amenity space and 240.70 square metres of outdoor amenity space.

Site Plan Control Application proposes the development of a 44-storey mixed-use building providing residential and commercial use at grade. The building will provide a total of 465 residential units, being a mix of studio, 1-bedroom, 2-bedroom, and 3-bedroom units. The building will also provide retail at grade. A paved access from Bayview Ave will be provided, leading to 2 levels of below-grade parking, providing a total of 350 parking spaces; as well as 526 bicycle parking spaces. The proposed development for an Education Centre and parking garage is located on an existing surface parking lot in the northwest corner of the property. A total GFA of 46,577 square metres is proposed; 17,750 square metres are for the 6-storey L-shaped Education Centre, and 28,827 square metres for the 6-storey parking garage (which includes 7 levels of parking, given parking located on the roof of the structure). 114 surface parking spaces are included with the proposed development, and 900 parking spaces are proposed within the parking garage, for a total of 1,014 spaces. These spaces contribute to a total parking supply of 1,599 parking spaces on the site

Bousfields Inc. has submitted an application for Zoning By-law Amendment.  The application proposes three mixed-use buildings with heights of 25, 30 and 37 storeys. The proposed development would be deployed in 2 phases with phase 1 consisting of the 37-storey building and phase 2 consisting of the 25 and 30 storeys buildings. The application proposes 935 residential units with a mix of unit types resulting in a residential gross floor area of 67,992 square metres. The application also proposes 522 square metres of retail/commercial uses and an overall floor space index of 7.36 times. A total of 742 vehicular and 714 bicycle parking spaces and 3 type G and 6 type C loading spaces would be provided. The proposal would provide a new 930 square-metre public park at the western portion of the site, pedestrian connections and privately-owned publicly accessible open spaces. A related Subdivision Application No. 21 201457 ESC 21 SB to create 4 new blocks has also been submitted by the applicant.

Site Plan application for Phase 1 of the proposed development for the lands at 1900 Eglinton Ave E to permit two (2) mixed-use buildings on the southwest portion of the lands and 1,750 sq. m. of parkland (triangular park on the east side of Hakimi Ave). The proposed 45-storey (east) and 48-storey (west) buildings would consist of a total gross floor area (GFA) of 71,738 sq. m. (69.777 sq. m for residential uses and 1,961 sq. m for retail uses), 975 residential units and a floor space index (FSI) of approximately 8.77 times the application lands. Bousfields Inc. has submitted an application for Zoning By-law Amendment.  The application proposes three mixed-use buildings with heights of 25, 30 and 37 storeys. The proposed development would be deployed in 2 phases with phase 1 consisting of the 37-storey building and phase 2 consisting of the 25 and 30 storeys buildings. The application proposes 935 residential units with a mix of unit types resulting in a residential gross floor area of 67,992 square metres. The application also proposes 522 square metres of retail/commercial uses and an overall floor space index of 7.36 times. A total of 742 vehicular and 714 bicycle parking spaces and 3 type G and 6 type C loading spaces would be provided. The proposal would provide a new 930 square-metre public park at the western portion of the site, pedestrian connections and privately-owned publicly accessible open spaces. A related Subdivision Application No. 21 201457 ESC 21 SB to create 4 new blocks has also been submitted by the applicant.

Rezoning for proposed 40-storey tower with an 8-storey podium and three-levels of underground parking. Proposal for three buildings based on an approved Municipal Zoning Order. A total of 997 dwelling units, including 282 affordable rental dwelling units (approximately 28% of the total), a 955 square metre community space and 505 square metres of retail space are proposed. Building A (at the central northern part of the site) is a 34-storey tower with retail space on the ground floor and 314 dwelling units above. Building B (at the central southern part of the site) is a 31-storey tower with the existing machine shop building conserved and integrated into the base of the tower. Building B includes retail space and a community space on the ground floor and 282 affordable rental dwelling units above. Building C (at the northeastern corner of the site) is a 43-storey residential tower that includes 401 dwelling units. The existing cleaning room building at the northwestern corner of the site is proposed to be conserved, with the potential to include non-residential uses, but is not currently identified as part of the proposed development. The MZO is available at https://www.ontario.ca/laws/regulation/200595

The building would consist of a 4- to 9-storey pedestrian scale podium element and an architecturally distinctive 49-storey tower element containing 818 residential dwelling units (including 31 rental replacement units). A total gross floor area of 43,300 square metres is proposed, comprised of 43,203.2 square metres of residential gross floor area and 96.8 square metres of retail gross floor area.

Site Plan Application for new 342.36m² building on the subject lands to accommodate a Swiss Chalet restaurant with associated drive-through.

Site Plan application to permit a 11-storey residential building with 187 dwelling units and three levels of below-grade parking containing 218 vehicular parking spaces. The proposed total gross floor area is 14, 794 square metres, resulting in a floor space index of 3.5 times the area of the lot.
The proposed development consists of a 35-storey tower with a 6-storey mixed-use podium including retail use at grade, three (3) floors of office uses and 362 residential units.

Site Plan Control application for the southeast portion of the site (Phase 1) consisting of a 32-storey mixed-use building (102.5 metres) fronting onto Eglinton Ave E and a 48-storey residential building (149.5 metres) fronting onto Birchmount Rd with a shared 5 to 6-storey base building. A total gross floor area of 63,275 square metres (62,380 square metres for residential uses and 895 square metres for retail uses) and 1,009 residential units are proposed. The application proposes, 422 vehicle parking spaces (419 spaces for residential uses and 3 spaces for non-residential uses), 759 bicycle parking spaces (688 long-term spaces and 71 short-term spaces), and 3 loading spaces (2 Type 'C' spaces and 1 Type 'G' space).

Zoning By-law Amendment application to permit a mixed-use development at 1920-1940 Eglinton Ave E, 880-900 Warden Ave and 20-50 Ashtonbee Rd (Madison lands), including 11 tall buildings with heights that range from 18 to 48 storeys, two (2) mid-rise buildings with heights that range from six (6) to seven (7) storeys, new public streets, and public parkland. A total gross floor area of 305,863  square metres (289,472 square metres for residential uses; 8,901 square metres for office uses; and 5,979 square metres for retail uses) and 4,285 dwelling units are proposed. This application is related to Official Plan Amendment Application No. 19 115893 ESC 21 OZ.

Zoning By-law Amendment application to permit a 30-storey mixed-use building, containing 216 dwelling units and 320 square metres of non-residential GFA. The site includes a listed property on the Heritage Register which is proposed to be demolished.

Proposed Development consists of a new 31-storey (102 metres in height) residential building with 296 new condominium units and 26 rental replacement units for a total of 322 dwelling units. The total residential gross floor area (GFA) is 22,243 square metres (15.3 FSI). A total of 89 associated parking spaces and 322 bicycle parking spaces will be proposed. The Proposed Development will include the demolition and replacement of the existing 26 rental units.

The proposal features a 33-storey tall buiding and a 9-storey mid-rise building component, both connected by a 4-storey base building. It also includes a total gross floor area of 40,550 square metres including 350 square metres of retail at grade, 606 residential units, and a floor space index of 4.3 times the lot area.

Proposal to redevelop the Subject Lands and provide a 44-storey mixed-use building (exclusive of mechanical penthouse). The proposed development will have a total gross floor area of approximately 37,419 sq. m., with 288 sq. m of non-residential uses at grade. The total development will have a Floor Space Index of 8.79 times the lot area. A total of 555 residential units, 278 parking spaces, and 416 bicycle spaces are proposed.

Proposal to redevelop the Subject Lands and provide a 46-storey mixed-use building (exclusive of mechanical penthouse). The proposed development will have a total gross floor area of approximately 38,348 sq. m., with 500 sq. m of non-residential uses at grade. The total development will have a Floor Space Index of 9.05 times the lot area. A total of 578 residential units, 158 parking spaces, and 450 bicycle spaces are proposed.

Official Plan and Zoning By-law Amendment to increase the approved height and density onsite to accomodate additional purpose built and affordable rental dwelling units.  On Block 3, Tower 3A increases by 13 storeys and Tower 3B increases by 12 storeys for a total of 48 and 37 storeys, respectively. The proposal increases Block 4, Tower 4A by 12 storeys and Tower 4B by 11 storeys for a total of 44 and 34 storeys, respectively.  A total of  3,547 dwelling units across five blocks are proposed, 634 of which are already approved and under construction.
